title: Regulatory/ Medical Affairs Associate (Pharmaceutical)
description:
Job Description:

  • Provide support in the development of medical and scientific materials such as presentation slides, training content, FAQs, and briefing documents.
  • Coordinate and assist in the planning of scientific training programs for both internal teams and external stakeholders.
  • Conduct literature reviews and summarize clinical evidence, treatment guidelines, and scientific publications to support medical communications.
  • Review and ensure that all medical content is accurate, properly referenced, and aligned with internal compliance standards.
  • Assist in the preparation and compilation of product registration dossiers for submission to relevant health authorities.
  • Support company licensing processes, including applications, renewals, and variations (e.g., importer, wholesaler, and distributor licences).
  • Monitor regulatory timelines, submission schedules, and correspondence with regulatory authorities.
  • Maintain well-organized regulatory documentation, trackers, and filing systems.
  • Support medical engagement activities with healthcare professionals under appropriate supervision.
  • Assist in drafting scientific responses to medical information inquiries.
  • Coordinate logistics and materials for advisory boards, scientific conferences, and educational programs.
  • Maintain accurate records of medical interactions in compliance with regulatory and internal requirements.
  • Collaborate with commercial, regulatory, and operations teams to support medical-related initiatives.
  • Assist in preparing meeting agendas, documenting minutes, and tracking follow-up actions.
  • Provide support for ad hoc projects related to medical affairs, regulatory submissions, and clinical education.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, Pharmacy, Biomedical Science, Nursing, Medicine, or a related field.
  • Minimum 2 years of working experience in medical affairs, regulatory affairs, clinical research, or healthcare-related roles.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills

Interested applicants for the above advertised position(s), please kindly email an updated copy of your resume to: gs1@talenttradersg.com;


EA License No.: 13C6305

Registration No.: R23117856 CHOE KIN MUN


For candidate who applied for the advertised position is deemed to have consented to us that we may collect, use or disclose your personal information for purpose in connection with the services provided by us.
